# sense-of-style

> Diagnose and rewrite prose using Steven Pinker's Sense of Style principles. Scores 8 dimensions, flags line-level problems (nominalizations, passive voice, hedges, broken topic chains, clichés), and rewrites — preserving voice, killing bloat. Pairs with qa-loop.

For prose where the content is right but the writing is soft. PRDs, updates, posts, emails, essays. qa-loop fixes argument; sense-of-style fixes prose.

## Docs

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/derekcedarbaum2/sense-of-style/main/README.md): the 8 dimensions, when to use vs. not, install.
- [SKILL.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/derekcedarbaum2/sense-of-style/main/SKILL.md): the skill itself.
- [AGENTS.md](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/derekcedarbaum2/sense-of-style/main/AGENTS.md): operating protocol for agents installing this for an operator.
- [Examples](https://github.com/derekcedarbaum2/sense-of-style/tree/main/examples): annotated before-and-after rewrites.

## Related

- [qa-loop](https://github.com/derekcedarbaum2/qa-loop): pairs with sense-of-style. Run qa-loop first to fix the argument; then run sense-of-style on the prose.

## Optional

- [LICENSE](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/derekcedarbaum2/sense-of-style/main/LICENSE): MIT.
